title: HIVE Digital's Q4 Loss Analysis and Crypto Mining Accounting Reality
text: HIVE Digital Technologies reported a net loss of $0.34 per share in Q4 2025, compared to a net profit of $0.55 per share in Q4 2024. For the full fiscal year 2025, HIVE reported revenue of $115.3M (flat year-over-year), with adjusted EBITDA of $56.2M and 1,414 Bitcoin mined. The company's gross operating margin decreased in absolute terms to $25.1 million in fiscal 2025, compared to $37.5 million in the prior year. This decline was significantly impacted by the Bitcoin halving event that occurred in April 2024, which led to mining rewards being halved compared to the prior year. Despite these financial headwinds, HIVE expanded its hash rate from 4.7 to 11.5 exahash during the year and expects to reach 25 exahash by fall 2025. Additionally, HIVE's high-performance computing revenue increased close to 300% compared to the prior year. The analysis highlights the complexity of cryptocurrency mining accounting, with significant non-cash charges impacting reported earnings despite strong operational performance.
